title: Richly Spiced Egyptian Lamb Tagine
description: A resinous and molten Egyptian dish featuring paprika and shio-cure, perfect for a sick day, this richly spiced lamb tagine is a gluten-free friendly option that will warm your senses.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the paprika, shio-cure, cumin, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper.
Add 2 cups of water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Cook for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro.
